summary: Excellent soundboard mastering captures a stellar May‑77 Florida performance with monster Dancing in the Streets and a magical Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s sequence.
review: The show is hailed as one of the top dates of the May‑77 tour, featuring an inspired first set (Music Never Stopped, Sugaree, Lazy Lightning) and a second‑set suite that includes a “magical” Help > Slipknot! > Franklin’s and a cathartic Morning Dew.  Reviewers praise Jerry Garcia’s transcendent guitar work and the band’s flawless transitions, while noting that the new master‑reel SBD improves on earlier boomy lows and delivers an excellent listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviewers are enthusiastic, highlighting both superb musical performance and a high‑quality soundboard source; the few technical quirks are outweighed by the overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Earlier low‑gen SBD had boomy lows, but the 150935 master‑reel version smooths those issues and offers a clear, balanced mix.  A matrix version also exists.  Some minor clipping/editing is noted on the SBD, yet overall clarity and fidelity are praised.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set bursts out with high energy and tight jams, while the second set builds a seamless, almost narrative flow that peaks with the Help‑Slipknot‑Franklin’s suite and resolves beautifully in Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is described as supernatural, with superb middle sections, improvisational brilliance, and a beautiful melodic voice throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir delivers strong vocals (notably on Sugar Magnolia) and contributes solid rhythm work; his “Music” moment is highlighted.
